kept.text:
The Tesla Semi is fundamentally more efficient than diesel

Going over a mountain range consumes a massive amount of energy. A diesel truck wastes all that gravitational potential energy and burns up expensive brakes on the way down

The Tesla Semi recaptures that energy, feeds it straight back into the battery, and never overheats the brakes...essentially never using them

Add in the fact that electric motors turn ~95% of their energy into motion (while diesel wastes most of it as heat), and the aerodynamic design slices through the air like a bullet instead of a brick wall

You get faster uphill climbs, virtually zero brake maintenance, and a fraction of the cost per mile

Physics doesn’t lie... diesel has a massive downside

The entire future will be electric, and the Tesla Semi wins in every direction

kept.text:
🔥🚀 Tesla Spring Update 2026.14.3 is now rolling out to the 7th and 8th batches of vehicles (reaching ~61% of the fleet)!

Only bug fixes are expected compared to 2026.14.1 and .2.
Notice anything new? Feel free to share!

2026.14.1 changes:
✅ Self-Driving App [AI4]
With the new Self-Driving app, subscribe with a single tap, learn how to activate and use the feature, and view ongoing stats.

✅ "Hey Grok"
Launch Grok by saying "Hey Grok." Go to Grok > Settings to enable. You can also set location based reminders, like "remind me to pick up milk when I'm near home." Say "goodbye" to dismiss.

✅ Pet Mode
Choose and name your pet by going to Controls > Display > Customize Pet Mode.

✅ Visual Updates [New Model 3/Y]
The center display now features higher quality images of your Tesla, and an enhanced park scene environment.

✅ Trips
Trips are now in the Energy app, where you can create and manage multiple trips to track energy usage across different drives, routes, or conditions. Go to App Launcher > Energy > Trips. For quick access to the Trips Card, swipe left on the Media Card.

✅ Sketchpad
Unleash your inner artist with the enhanced Sketchpad, now supporting stickers and emojis. Save your sketches to access them in the Mobile App and share.

✅ Custom Wraps and License Plates [S/X]
Model S & X owners can now personalize their Tesla avatar with window tints, custom wraps & license plates.

✅ Blind Spot Warning Accent Lights
Accent lights now turn red when an object is in your blind spot and your turn signal is engaged, or when an approaching object is detected while parked. To disable, go to Controls > Lights > Blind Spot Warning Accent Lights.

✅ Immersive Sound Upgrade [New Model 3 & Y w/ premium audio]
Premium Immersive Sound uses advanced sound extraction to place the listener in front of a detailed soundstage within an Immersive space. This enhancement works with all streaming sources.

✅ Music App Queuing
Swipe right on tracks in Apple Music or Spotify to add them to the queue. In Apple Music, tap and hold on a track to add or remove from Favorites. In Spotify, swipe left to add or remove from Liked Songs.

✅ Automatic Software Updates
Your Tesla can now automatically install downloaded software updates overnight. When enabled, updates will install while your Tesla is parked and not in use, ensuring you always have the latest features and improvements.
To enable, go to Controls > Software > Automatically Install Updates.

✅ Dashcam Viewer Updates
Recent Dashcam footage can now extend up to 24 hours, and you can save any clip for permanent storage on your Tesla or on your phone by going to App Launcher > Dashcam Viewer.
Clips downloaded to your phone now include details such as speed, steering wheel angle, and Self-Driving state.

✅ Weather Maps Improvements
Weather maps now show snow and rain with improved colors that make it easier to distinguish between precipitation types. You can also view the past hour of weather data to see how conditions have been changing along your route.

✅ Supercharger Pricing Filter
You can now choose to route only to Superchargers with Tesla pricing. When enabled, Trip Planner will avoid locations where Tesla does not set charging prices or accept Free Supercharging credits.

✅ Keyboard
Your selected keyboard language will also select the language used for dictation. Tap the international keyboard button to change keyboard and dictation language, without changing voice command language. Tap the mic icon in the keyboard to dictate.

✅ Rear Display
Passengers in rear seats can now view and interact with maps on the rear display while your Tesla navigates a route. Tap the maps icon from the rear display to follow along.

✅ Security Improvements

✅ Larger icons on the bottom bar [undocumented]

✅ Animated badge in software menu [undocumented]

✅ Audio source icon next to the volume slider [undocumented]

•⁠  ⁠2010: First private company to launch, orbit and recover a spacecraft

•⁠  ⁠2015: First orbital-class rocket landing (Falcon 9, ORBCOMM-2)
•⁠  ⁠2017: First reuse of an orbital booster (B1021, SES-10)
•⁠  ⁠2018: Falcon Heavy debut with the Tesla Roadster
•⁠  ⁠2020: First private company to fly humans to the ISS (Demo-2)
•⁠  ⁠Starlink: global internet from low Earth orbit, 12M+ subscribers
•⁠  ⁠Starship: from first flight to mid-air booster catches in 3 years
•⁠  ⁠Today: SpaceX accounts for the majority of all global payload mass to orbit

kept.text:
7 years ago, I saw Elon Musk at an event.

I didn't speak to him. I didn't shake his hand. I just watched him cross the floor.

The aura was different.

I couldn't explain it then. I can now.

When I was a kid, I used to watch ants build colonies. I'd sit there for hours. One ant would walk a path. Then another would walk the same path. Then another. By sunset, the whole colony moved along that single line.

None of them questioned why.

The first ant chose by accident. Every ant after followed the accident as if it was law. The path became sacred. The path was just one ant's random decision crystallized into tradition.

That's most people.

Every other person in that room was an ant on the path. Brilliant ants. Wealthy ants. Successful ants. But ants. Each of them carrying invisible inheritance: assumptions made by other people in other times for reasons nobody alive remembers.

He wasn't.

Aerospace told him rockets cost 65 million dollars. He asked what they were made of. Aluminum, titanium, copper, carbon fiber. Material cost: 2 percent of the price. The other 98 percent was tradition pretending to be physics. He built rockets from raw materials and cut costs by 10x.

Auto industry told him electric cars couldn't compete. He asked why. Batteries were expensive because the industry bought assembled batteries at markup. Materials cost less. He made his own batteries. Tesla became worth more than every legacy automaker combined.

Banking told him payments were a solved problem. He asked who solved it. Banks did, in 1950, for a different world. He built PayPal. Sold it for 180 million dollars at 28 years old.

This is the pattern.

While everyone else inherits the path from accidental ants, he asks why the path exists. While everyone else optimizes within the assumption, he questions the assumption. While everyone else competes on execution, he competes on perception of reality.

The aura wasn't charisma. It wasn't height or voice or eye contact.

It was the absence of inherited weight.

Most people you meet are carrying centuries of unexamined assumptions. They believe what their teachers believed who believed what their teachers believed who believed what some ant decided by accident in 1923. The weight is invisible but it's real. You feel it when you stand near them.

He wasn't carrying any of it.

That's what genius is. Not higher IQ. Not more information. Not better memory. Just the willingness to question every assumption others accept and the discipline to rebuild reality from atoms instead of inheriting it from convention.

7 years later, I run my entire life this way.

Every decision: what is this actually made of? What am I assuming? What would this look like if I designed it from atoms instead of borrowing it from people who designed it for situations that no longer exist?

The willingness to do this work separates the ants from the architects.

Most people will never do it. It's slower upfront. It's lonelier. It requires tolerating the discomfort of disagreeing with consensus when consensus feels safe.

But compound interest on first principles thinking over decades is what built SpaceX. It's what built Tesla. It's what built every empire worth studying.

The man crossing that floor wasn't magic.

He was just one of the few humans alive actually thinking for himself while the rest of the world rented their conclusions from accidental ants.

That's the aura.

That's what it feels like.

That's what it costs.

NEWS: Tesla Semi starts a port drayage pilot with MDB Transportation in Southern California.

MDB, a Los Angeles logistics company, is running the Tesla Semi for three weeks on busy freight routes around the LA port area. The truck will move containers between marine terminals, rail yards and distribution hubs.

"We're proud to be operating the Tesla Semi, the future of freight isn't a concept, it's in motion," said Haig Melkonyan, Director of Operations at MDB Transportation.

Port drayage is one of the best fits for electric trucks because the routes are short, predictable and run multiple times a day. This makes charging much easier to plan compared to long-haul trips. The pilot will track energy use, turnaround speed and driver feedback.

The Semi being tested can haul up to 82,000 pounds, drive up to 500 miles per charge, and use less than 2 kWh per mile. With a Megacharger, it can recover 60% of its range in about 30 minutes.

Real-world results so far have been strong. A recent Texas trial by Mone Transport ran over 4,700 miles and hit 1.64 kWh per mile, beating Tesla's own targets. Hight Logistics has also added Semis for port work.

With high-volume Semi production now underway, more fleets are starting to put the truck into real daily service.

TL;DR 🚨 Tesla’s Secret Geometric Fix: How a simple cut unlocks the million-mile battery ⚡️

🔋 The biggest threat to modern battery life isn't just chemistry, it's pure geometry. Traditional battery materials are cut into massive rectangles and rolled tightly together, but this blunt, straight edge creates a microscopic cliff inside the core that concentrates physical pressure, causes an internal kink, and eventually crushes the battery from the inside out.

📐 To solve this, Tesla's new patent completely abandons the straight cut, shaping the ends of the battery layers—including the underlying metal foil, the conductive adhesive, and the active chemical paste—into precise chevrons and smooth curves. This cleverly staggers the final physical drop-off, diffusing the mechanical stress throughout the entire roll so no single weak spot or buckling zone can ever form.

🕸️ These complex new geometric shapes are held together by an invisible web of dry, Teflon-like fibers that give the active battery powder the structural strength to hold its form without fraying at the borders.

✂️ To actually craft these unique edges without crushing the fragile materials at factory speeds, Tesla uses a brilliant subtractive peeling trick—simply leaving the edge unglued and lifting away the scrap powder instead of chopping it with heavy machinery.

🤖 Having a perfectly clean, shaped boundary also gives high-speed robotic cameras a clear line of sight to perfectly align the overlapping battery layers, which prevents catastrophic short circuits and stops the factory from throwing away defective cells.

🧩 Ultimately, this purely physical redesign acts as the ultimate mechanical failsafe, ensuring that all of Tesla's highly advanced dry battery chemistry can actually survive the violent high-speed winding process intact.

💰 By fixing this hidden microscopic flaw, Tesla is immediately boosting 4680 gigafactory manufacturing yields and laying the durable physical foundation required for 1,000,000-mile Robotaxis and massive grid storage systems that can reliably cycle for decades.

kept.text:
TL;DR 🚨 Tesla’s "Live MRI" for Supercomputers in a nutshell 🔬

📉 Finding one dead chip inside a massive AI supercomputer used to require engineers to blindly sift through endless text logs, often forcing them to scrap entire multi-million-dollar hardware racks over a single microscopic fault.

🚨 To solve this, Tesla created a "Live MRI" software tool to visually diagnose and fix broken AI chips on the fly, saving millions of dollars in dead silicon.

🌡️ The system works by using tiny "watchdog" sensors that sit directly next to the main processors, existing solely to monitor the hardware's physical health in real time.

📡 A hidden networking nervous system then streams these real-time vital signs from the watchdogs directly to the operator's screen.

⚡ Instead of tedious log files, this data is rendered into color-coded diagnostic heat maps that show the exact path of cascading errors instantly.

💡 Using this new visual dashboard, engineers can draw digital boundaries around faulty chips to isolate and analyze them without unplugging anything.

🖥️ From there, operators can zoom in on the problematic sector to tweak power or speed settings without ever needing to reboot the whole server rack.

🏗️ To support this intense, localized testing, heavy-duty power lines and liquid cooling prevent the hardware from melting when pushed to its limits.

🛡️ During these adjustments, built-in software safeguards act like a smart spellchecker, strictly preventing human errors that could accidentally crash the entire machine.

🧩 Once the issue is mapped, the software simply builds a digital detour to route mathematical traffic around the dead spot, saving the board from the trash.

🛠️ Beyond just fixing active errors, engineers can even digitally "clone" hardware to test connections in a virtual simulator before physically plugging it into the supercomputer.

🚀 Ultimately, this visual tool turns an 8-hour diagnostic nightmare into a 5-minute fix, rescuing slightly damaged hardware and accelerating Tesla's AI timeline.

NEWS: Tesla Model 3 driver walks away unharmed after a Dodge Ram pickup landed on top of the car in a three-vehicle crash in Washington.

The crash happened on May 1 at about 6:01 p.m. at the intersection of Northeast 99th Street and Northeast 130th Avenue in Orchards, Washington. Clark County deputies and Vancouver Fire Engine 11 arrived within three minutes of the 911 calls.

When they got to the scene, deputies found a black Dodge Ram pickup resting on top of a gray Tesla Model 3 sedan, with a white Ford Econoline van blocking the westbound lane about 60 feet away.

Investigators say the Dodge Ram ran a red light heading west on Northeast 99th Street, smashing into the driver's side of the Ford Econoline that had a green light. The pickup then slid sideways into the eastbound lane and crashed into the Tesla Model 3, which was stopped at the light.

The Dodge Ram driver showed signs of impairment, and a DUI investigation followed. The Ford van driver was hurt and taken to a nearby hospital. The Tesla driver walked away with no physical injuries.

The Traffic Homicide Unit was called in and the road was shut for about five hours during the investigation. Detectives are now asking for help from anyone who saw the Dodge Ram driving before the crash.

kept.text:
SpaceX Musk compensation incentive goal: 100 TW

"operates data centers in space that provide at least 100 terawatts of compute capacity."

Remember the famous Terafab chart showing 1 TW labeled "Expected compute demand from Tesla + SpaceX"?

The Musk milestone is 100 TW.

Have they gone insane?

Actually no. We could hit 100 TW of demand in less than 10 years.

Today global AI data center power capacity is about 30 GW. Let's check this against growth in the CPU growth era, 1980-2000's. The growth rate then was 58% per year (growing at 1.58x per year). By that measure, 30 GW will grow to 1 TW in 7.7 years (2034). And 100 TW at 17.7 years (2043).  BUT, AI compute demand is growing much faster than the CPU-era growth.  About ~3.4× per year. That’s more than twice as fast as the historical 58% rate.  

So the demand for 1 TW equates to only 3 years and 100 TW only 7 years. Even in the most optimistic case (e.g., 1.5× FLOPS/W efficiency gains every year from Blackwell/Rubin-class chips), we still hit 1 TW in ~4.3 years and 100 TW in ~10 years.

And I think inference compute demand is going to accelerate from here!
